events.label: reasoning
events.text: We are assessing duct installation at rough-complete stage. The observed condition: unsealed joints (no mastic/foil tape - cloth duct tape is the classic wrong answer). This is non-compliant per IMC 603 and SMACNA standards. The work stage is rough-complete. The condition is non-compliant and installed-defective. Percent complete maybe like 70% because joints not sealed? Decision: fail. Risk: medium? Could be high if duct tape was used, but it says "no mastic/foil tape - cloth duct tape is the classic wrong answer, great ID item" meaning they used cloth duct tape? Actually it says "unsealed joints (no mastic/foil tape - cloth duct tape is the classic wrong answer, great ID item)". So they didn't use mastic or foil tape, but they may have used cloth duct tape, which is wrong. So yes, fail. references: IMC 603, SMACNA. confidence high. component_conditions: ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"]. findings: ["unsealed duct joints using cloth duct tape instead of mastic or foil tape"]. actions: ["Remove cloth duct tape and seal joints with mastic or UL-listed foil tape"]. percent_complete maybe 70% because joints need re-sealing. value not relevant. sound_source etc not relevant. order, workable empty. rationale: Cloth duct tape is not an approved sealant per IMC 603 and SMACNA; it deteriorates and fails to seal. decision fail. risk: medium because it can lead to leakage and energy loss, but not immediately dangerous. risk medium. event_time and rate not applicable. So produce JSON.

  "rationale": "Cloth duct tape is not an approved sealant per IMC 603 and SMACNA; it deteriorates, fails to provide air-tight seal, and leads to leakage. Duct joints must be sealed with mastic or foil tape systems rated for HVAC ductwork.",
  "references": ["IMC 603", "SMACNA"]
}
